region_preference: invisible - processed food, HoReCa, cheap blends, PDS-adjacent
source: Business Standard citing industry figures (37% share); SEA 2023-24 imports CPO 69.7 + RBD palmolein 19.31 = 89 LMT; NITI/USDA domestic CPO 3.8 LMT
note: Largest single oil consumed and ~entirely imported; not sold as branded consumer oil - used in namkeen, bakery, vanaspati, cheap loose/blended oil. Share applied to ~255 LMT total consumption.

source: Business Standard industry figures (20% share); SEA 2023-24 soy oil imports 34.41 LMT; USDA MY23/24 domestic soy oil ~20 LMT
note: Roughly half imported (Argentina/Brazil), half domestic crush. Record 5.47 MT soy oil imported in 2024-25 MY.

source: Business Standard industry figures (13% share); SEA 2023-24 sunflower oil imports 35.06 LMT (Russia/Ukraine/Argentina)
note: Almost entirely imported; urban health-positioned. Domestic sunflower seed output tiny (~2.5-2.8 LMT seed).

source: USDA MY23/24 domestic rapeseed-mustard oil 4.2 MMT = 42 LMT (largest indigenous oil); consumption share 14% Business Standard
note: India's largest DOMESTIC oil; ~100% domestically produced, near-zero import. Kachi ghani/pungent grades are a preference premium, not a palm price competitor.

source: USDA MY23/24 domestic groundnut oil 1.2 MMT = 12 LMT; ~5% consumption share (residual)
note: ~100% domestic. ~half of groundnut crop goes to direct food/seed/export (table/confectionery), only ~25-30% crushed for oil. Premium oil (~Rs 200+/kg retail).

source: USDA/industry ~10.3 LMT domestic rice bran oil; India world #1 in rice bran oil
note: Secondary source; byproduct of ~130 MT paddy milling. NMEO names it a collection-efficiency pillar. See domestic-procurement-stability-layer.md.

source: USDA MY23/24 cottonseed oil ~1.3 MMT = 13 LMT (~36% of secondary-source oil)
note: Byproduct of cotton; largest secondary-source oil. Mostly used in blends and vanaspati.
Regional, near-100% domestic; culinary in Kerala, otherwise hair/industrial.

region_preference: Sesame: South (esp Tamil Nadu), Bengal, Gujarat; plus corn, olive, niger
source: Residual of ~255 LMT total after named oils; SEA/Bonafide category list
note: Sesame/gingelly is a specialty premium oil; corn/olive small urban niches.

source: Consumption ~25-26 MT (250-260 LMT) NITI/industry; domestic output 12.18 MT (121.8 LMT) NITI 2023-24 = 44% of demand; imports ~160 LMT = ~57% import dependency (SEA 2024-25 imports 16 MT, Rs 1.61 lakh cr)
note: Per-capita ~19.7 kg/yr (NITI) rising to ~24 kg (Business Standard 2025). Totals rounded; type-level LMT derived from reported shares x ~255 LMT and cross-checked to SEA import volumes and USDA domestic-output breakdown - treat type splits as reported/indicative, totals as verified.
